core_payload.content:
# Key Agents Governance Lane

## 1. Boundary
Key Agents Governance Lane owns the critique, branch-discipline, and acceptance-boundary workers that keep the wider agent system from confusing output with trusted change. This sub-hub exists so governance pressure remains visible as its own lane instead of dissolving into maintenance or runtime folklore.

## 2. What Belongs Here
This sub-hub groups `branch-steward-agent`, `validation-gatekeeper-agent`, and `review-agent`. Together they define branch literacy, acceptance boundaries, critique authority, and the explicit review pressure that decides whether agent output is promotable, fixable, or blocked.

## 3. What Does Not Belong Here
This sub-hub does not own user interaction, mining, planning decomposition, or background swarm execution. Those belong to `user-lane`, `mining-lane`, `planning-lane`, `stewardship-lane`, and `swarm-lane`.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Key Agents Governance Lane groups the critique and trust-boundary workers of N1Hub: branch stewardship, validation gatekeeping, and review. It keeps governance pressure explicit and separate from maintenance, planning, mining, and swarm execution lanes. This matters because trusted change depends on visible acceptance boundaries, branch discipline, and criticism that can block unsafe promotion, route uncertain work into review, and keep the broader agent system from confusing generated output with validated repository truth.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Minted as part of key-agents hub decomposition to reduce parent link density from 28 outbound to ≤9.
